purpose: Isolate CPU-swapped AdamW state movement and update latency before spending a full 4xMI350 canary run on optimizer swap knobs.
read: Pinned CPU moment buffers work on MI350 and give a small warm-step win in this optimizer-only shape: FP32 states improve from 19.82 ms to 19.47 ms, and BF16/param states improve from 11.40 ms to 10.94 ms. BF16/param moments remain much faster because they move half the state bytes, but FP32 moments are the more faithful Ascend AdamW swap contract. The full MBS4 run remains Python-loop dominated because it visits many FSDP/EP parameter shards, so the next throughput rung is a fused/pipelined C++ or HIP swap-update path, not more Python knob tuning.
validation: ROCm 1xMI350 benchmark smoke passed with pinned FP32 CPU states.
Targeted ROCm container unittest passed: 4 swap_opt_states_cpu tests.
